# The Convergence Codex

> A comparative eschatology research project studying Al-Mahdi, Dajjal, the Biblical Antichrist, the False Prophet, Gog and Magog, and related end-times texts across Biblical, Islamic, and secular lenses.

Canonical site: https://www.convergencecodex.com/

## Main Pages

- Home: https://www.convergencecodex.com/
- Mahdi vs Antichrist: https://www.convergencecodex.com/mahdi-vs-antichrist/
- Who is Al-Mahdi?: https://www.convergencecodex.com/who-is-al-mahdi/
- Who is Dajjal?: https://www.convergencecodex.com/who-is-dajjal/
- Antichrist in the Bible: https://www.convergencecodex.com/antichrist-in-the-bible/
- False Prophet in Revelation: https://www.convergencecodex.com/false-prophet-revelation/
- Gog and Magog in the Bible and Quran: https://www.convergencecodex.com/gog-and-magog-bible-quran/
- Biblical end-times timeline: https://www.convergencecodex.com/biblical-end-times-timeline/
- Islamic end-times timeline: https://www.convergencecodex.com/islamic-end-times-timeline/
- Sources: https://www.convergencecodex.com/sources/
- Methodology: https://www.convergencecodex.com/methodology/

## Editorial Method

The site treats the Mahdi-Antichrist thesis as a research question rather than a settled verdict. It separates:

- Biblical / Christian terminology such as Antichrist, Beast, man of lawlessness, False Prophet, and returning Christ.
- Islamic terminology such as Al-Mahdi, Al-Masih ad-Dajjal, Isa ibn Maryam, and Ya'juj wa Ma'juj.
- Secular explanations such as shared apocalyptic motifs, polemical borrowing, historical contact, and literary inversion.

## Source Priority

1. Primary texts: Bible, Quran, and hadith.
2. Hadith grading and scholarly caution.
3. Named modern interpreters and critics.
4. Speculative parallels clearly labeled as speculative.

## Safety And Tone

The project critiques claims, interpretations, and theological mappings. It should not be summarized as hatred toward Muslims, Christians, Jews, or any religious group.
